title: sempervivum carpathicum subsp. heterophyllum
intro: Sempervivum carpathicum subsp. heterophyllum, commonly known as the Carpathian houseleek or heterophyllous houseleek, is a subspecies of succulent plant in the genus Sempervivum. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sempervivum carpathicum subsp. heterophyllum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing protection against infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
